dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I thought I was managing. I thought the worst was over. I was wrong.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: His old business partner came to {location} demanding payment on a debt I didn't know existed. Says my husband borrowed {quest_item} months before he died and never settled the account. {target_npc} tried to mediate, but the partner is threatening to take our home if I don't pay. The only way to gather enough is from the old supply cache {location_hint}, but {enemy_type} have taken over the area since the route went unused. I can't lose this house: it's all I have left of him.
dialogueChunks.outro: Grief wasn't enough, apparently. Now I'm fighting to keep a roof over my head too.

playerResponses.text: Nobody's taking your home. I'll get what you need.
Debt collectors circling a widow. Charming. I'll handle it.
Financial disputes aren't something I can solve with a sword.

dialogueChunks.intro: The garden is growing again, {player_name}. But something is trying to destroy it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The same {enemy_type} that took my daughter have been raiding the outskirts of {location} again. They trampled through the garden two nights ago: not for food, just destruction. {target_npc} set up a watch, but we're short-handed and the creatures come from different directions each time. I need more {quest_item} to rebuild the beds and proper fencing materials from {location_hint}. And someone needs to deal with the nest those creatures are coming from before they destroy everything we've replanted.
dialogueChunks.outro: I won't let them take this from me too. She grew this garden from bare dirt, and it's going to survive.

playerResponses.text: They won't touch that garden again. I'll find their nest.
Defending a garden from monsters. Strange work, but meaningful.
The garden isn't worth getting killed over.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I found something in his pack that changes everything.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Hidden in a false bottom of my brother's delivery satchel was a letter. He knew the road {location_hint} was dangerous. He'd been warned about the {enemy_type} and went anyway because the neighboring settlement was desperate. He wrote, 'If something happens to me, make sure the {quest_item} gets through. These people are counting on it.' {target_npc} read the letter and hasn't stopped crying. Now there are reports that the settlement he was trying to reach is in worse shape than ever. I need to honor his wish: gather the supplies and complete the delivery.
dialogueChunks.outro: He knew the risk and chose to go anyway. The least we can do is finish what he died trying to do.

playerResponses.text: He was braver than he knew. I'll make sure his sacrifice counts.
A man who walked into danger for strangers deserves to have his mission completed.
Honoring the dead by risking the living. I understand, but I can't.
